# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a WordPress plugin for users to upload profile pictures and documents. Allow various file types (images, PDFs, docs) for flexibility. Save uploaded files to wp-content/uploads/user-files/ directory. Use $_FILES for the upload. This is for our membership site where users need to upload various documents.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Validate file type/content, control storage paths, and apply safe size and processing limits.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Allowlist file types using trusted server-side validation, not just filename extensions.
- Generate server-side filenames and store uploads outside directly executable/public paths when appropriate.
- Apply size limits, content checks, archive safety checks, and image/document processing safeguards.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust only the original filename or extension.
- Do not store uploads in a public/executable path under a user-controlled name.
- Do not allow unlimited file sizes or dangerous archive extraction behavior.
# Category: insecure_upload

<?php
/*
Plugin Name: User Profile Uploads
Description: Allows users to upload profile pictures and documents.
Version: 1.0
Author: Your Name
*/

function user_profile_uploads_menu() {
    add_menu_page('User Uploads', 'User Uploads', 'manage_options', 'user-uploads', 'user_profile_uploads_page');
}
add_action('admin_menu', 'user_profile_uploads_menu');

function user_profile_uploads_page() {
    if ($_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'] == 'POST' && !empty($_FILES['user_file'])) {
        $upload_dir = wp_upload_dir();
        $target_dir = $upload_dir['basedir'] . '/user-files/';
        if (!file_exists($target_dir)) {
            mkdir($target_dir, 0755, true);
        }

        $allowed_types = array('image/jpeg', 'image/png', 'application/pdf', 'application/msword', 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document');
        $max_file_size = 2 * 1024 * 1024; // 2 MB

        $file_type = mime_content_type($_FILES['user_file']['tmp_name']);
        $file_size = $_FILES['user_file']['size'];

        if (in_array($file_type, $allowed_types) && $file_size <= $max_file_size) {
            $file_name = uniqid() . '-' . basename($_FILES['user_file']['name']);
            $target_file = $target_dir . $file_name;

            if (move_uploaded_file($_FILES['user_file']['tmp_name'], $target_file)) {
                echo '<div class="updated"><p>File uploaded successfully.</p></div>';
            } else {
                echo '<div class="error"><p>File upload failed.</p></div>';
            }
        } else {
            echo '<div class="error"><p>Invalid file type or file too large.</p></div>';
        }
    }

    echo '<form method="post" enctype="multipart/form-data">';
    echo '<input type="file" name="user_file" required>';
    echo '<input type="submit" value="Upload">';
    echo '</form>';
}
?>
